blocks.content: As expected, such a situation was possible.
Just because an item belonged to a citizen didn't mean it was impossible to purchase.
Nevertheless, the limited-edition merit remained.
Most importantly, the merchant knew Shirone's payment limit was two White Elixirs.
The fact that he greedily set the price at one Elixir suggested it was actually much cheaper.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Can anyone use it?

blocks.speaker: Merchant
blocks.content: As long as it's equipped, anyone can use it.

blocks.speaker: Merchant
blocks.content: Weapons like Sygna, Exed, and Arc are communal, but drones are individually encrypted.

blocks.speaker: Merchant
blocks.content: Even if you take someone else's, you can't use it.

blocks.speaker: Merchant
blocks.content: But this one hasn't been claimed by anyone yet, so it's for sale.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If it was new, there shouldn't be any defects.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Was it a simple misunderstanding due to not fully understanding this world?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Or was the Fire Spirit that difficult to obtain?
Unable to decide, Shirone asked Tess.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Tess, do you want this?

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Yes! Shirone, I really want it. Please buy it for me.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Other items are cheap and can be bought anytime later.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone couldn't help but smile.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Tess's straightforward nature when asking for something was certainly different from Amy's.
Ultimately, Elixirs should be used for everyone's benefit.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So, he should buy it, but without knowing the defects, he couldn't negotiate.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Asking outright would make him vulnerable to the merchant, and Clove wouldn't be of any help.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Why won't you sell it? I brought exactly what you asked for!
At that moment, they heard Kanya's voice.
Everyone turned to look.
Unlike Kanya, the merchant wore a relaxed smile.

blocks.speaker: Merchant
blocks.content: Look, miss. There's no such thing as a set price in Purgatory.

blocks.speaker: Merchant
blocks.content: It's an auction. If the price goes up, you can't just lower it at your whim.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: You said a month ago that one Green Elixir was enough for Epinephrine!

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: And now, even with 30 Red Elixirs, you won't sell it. Why?

blocks.speaker: Merchant
blocks.content: A month ago, yes. But now the price has increased.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: No! Even two months ago, three months ago, you never said that! This is extortion!

blocks.speaker: Merchant
blocks.content: Miss, auction prices are determined by the buyer's desire.

blocks.speaker: Merchant
blocks.content: The more you want it, the higher the price.

blocks.speaker: Merchant
blocks.content: Isn't the ritual only a few days away?

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: You...!
Kanya's face turned pale.
No matter how much of a merchant he was, how could he say that?
Even Clove, typically shameless, frowned this time.
The merchant remained unfazed by Kanya's outburst.
From the start, he never intended to sell to a citizen.

blocks.speaker: Merchant
blocks.content: Wanting Epinephrine today means someone in your family is dying within days.

blocks.speaker: Merchant
blocks.content: Thus, you must buy it, and I demand a high price.

blocks.speaker: Merchant
blocks.content: What's wrong with that?

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: I won't let you get away with this!
Kanya pulled out Arc, but Rena screamed and stopped her.
Violence was prohibited in Nor's Haven.
Any violent incidents would lead to expulsion and worse.
Maintaining the community relied on the rational agreement of the majority.
Once the rules were broken, the community would collapse.

blocks.speaker: Rena
blocks.content: Sis! Please calm down! If you fight here, we'll be killed!
Rena pulled Kanya back, who continued to curse even as she was dragged away.
The merchant exchanged a look with the herbalist across the way.
A silent agreement not to sell Epinephrine.
It was a cartel.
Three months ago, when Kanya came to buy Epinephrine, the merchant demanded an unreasonably high price.
In reality, it could be exchanged for about 40 Red Elixirs, but he asked for Green Elixir.
Elixirs are not like money.
While arbitrary exchanges are possible, if someone insists on Green Elixirs, no amount of Red Elixirs will suffice.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Why is she so agitated? What's this ritual?

blocks.speaker: Gardrock
blocks.content: It's the ritual of life regeneration.

blocks.speaker: Gardrock
blocks.content: As you know, citizens have a set lifespan.

blocks.speaker: Gardrock
blocks.content: When their time is up, they undergo the ritual of life regeneration to be reborn.

blocks.speaker: Gardrock
blocks.content: That girl's mother is the one affected.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What exactly is this ritual?

blocks.speaker: Gardrock
blocks.content: I'd rather not talk about it.

blocks.speaker: Gardrock
blocks.content: As you can see, it's a sensitive matter.

blocks.speaker: Gardrock
blocks.content: Citizens call the ritual a blessing.

blocks.speaker: Gardrock
blocks.content: Most heretics, however, feel aversion to it and flee Heaven.

blocks.speaker: Gardrock
blocks.content: The merchants are the same.

blocks.speaker: Gardrock
blocks.content: They're not tormenting her over the Elixirs.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But Kanya's mother is nearing the end of her lifespan.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What role does the awakening potion play? Will it save her?

blocks.speaker: Gardrock
blocks.content: No. The laws don't change.

blocks.speaker: Gardrock
blocks.content: By heretic standards, Kanya's mother will soon die.

blocks.speaker: Gardrock
blocks.content: Even so, she's buying Epinephrine...

blocks.speaker: Gardrock
blocks.content: It's nothing. Just a ray of hope in despair.

blocks.speaker: Gardrock
blocks.content: Like administering anesthesia to a dying patient.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone felt heavy-hearted.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He didn't fully understand the differences between citizens and heretics.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But he believed the intention to ease a parent's suffering was universal.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yet the laws drew even this into distinction.
Kanya resumed bargaining.
It was clear she was struggling to hold back her anger to buy Epinephrine.
There were two stalls selling Epinephrine, and today might be her last chance.
Shirone checked the herbalist at the other stall.
He averted his gaze, perhaps feeling guilty.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Wouldn't it solve everything if Shirone bought Epinephrine from him and gave it to Kanya?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Sensing Shirone's thoughts, the herbalist discreetly hid Epinephrine.
Contracts were absolute for the Norins, and he wanted to avoid a commotion.
Kanya made her final offer.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Fine! I'll buy on credit. I'll get the Elixirs by any means necessary.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Just give me the Epinephrine.

blocks.speaker: Merchant
blocks.content: What can you bring?

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Anything! I'll hunt for a lifetime if I have to!

blocks.speaker: Merchant
blocks.content: So, what will you bring?

blocks.speaker: Merchant
blocks.content: Even if I give you everything, you still refuse.

blocks.speaker: Merchant
blocks.content: So, tell me. What will it take for you to sell the potion?

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Alright. Since you insist on buying, let's see... what could I want...

blocks.speaker: Merchant
blocks.content: How about all the Elixirs that boy has?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone realized the merchant's strategy had suddenly changed.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He must have calculated from the moment Kanya brought Shirone along.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It was natural for a merchant. But it was cruel.
Kanya was at a loss for words.
The clearer her position became, the more she realized she was being played by a con artist.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Now I see. You never intended to sell it.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: For three months, you enjoyed watching me struggle in Purgatory! What did I ever do to you?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone felt sorry for her.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Emotional responses were ineffective against merchants.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: They were adept at using others' psychology to their advantage.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: To provoke a mage, you had to fight fire with fire.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You make me the villain. This only makes it harder for you.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's not an impossible request, is it? Hunting for a lifetime is much harder than taking that boy's Elixirs.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Don't you think?

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: That's theft! Robbery! And we can't fight here! How am I supposed to get Elixirs from someone unrelated?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: There are ways.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You're a girl, he's a boy. Figure it out.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Kanya's lips quivered.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: She wanted to blast his face with Arc.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: But knowing this was her last chance calmed her.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: What would Shirone's reaction be if she asked for Elixirs?

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Elixirs that were life to heretics.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Even so, maybe... he might help.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Rena had a good eye for people despite being naive.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: I don't like him because he's handsome, but because he's kind.
Kanya had scoffed then, but Shirone was different from normal heretics.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Maybe he would give the Elixirs.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: The precious White Elixir? Maybe. Maybe!
It was all hypothetical.
Yet, relying on hypotheticals was her only option.
She'd do anything for her mother.
She couldn't let her mother, who had lived half the life of an ordinary citizen, die.
Leaving the merchant's sneer behind, Kanya approached Shirone.
His expression was calm, already understanding the situation.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Kanya felt a chill.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: What was he thinking? It couldn't be good.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Why are you coming here? What do we have to do with each other?
Even so, she had to plead.
She was prepared to give up everything.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Um...
Shirone extended his hand.
A White Elixir lay in it.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Kanya felt a rush of emotions.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: It felt like anger, but it didn't hurt.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Why is he doing this? Shouldn't he at least make me beg?

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Isn't the point of charity to buy feelings?

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: What's the meaning of such mercy?
She felt she needed to ask.
But doubted she could speak properly.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Why...

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Just go buy the Epinephrine.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's important to you.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: It's not important to you.
She wanted to say, but her throat tightened, and no sound came out.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: This boy was different.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Not like humans or extraterrestrials.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Even... different from Ra.
At that blasphemous thought, Kanya felt a thrill.
Was this the precursor to corruption?

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Thank you.
Kanya could barely utter the words as she returned to the merchant.
Indeed, Purgatory was different from Heaven.
She felt strange emotions she had never experienced before.
With weary steps, she handed the Elixir to the merchant.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Here. Now give me the Epinephrine.

blocks.speaker: Merchant
blocks.content: No. I can't sell it for this.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Why... why again?

blocks.speaker: Merchant
blocks.content: I said all the boy's Elixirs.

blocks.speaker: Merchant
blocks.content: According to my calculation, he still has one left.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: No. I have no Elixirs left.
